title: Programming Fundamentals 1
description: First course in programming fundamentals. Critical-tracking course for UF Computer Science CLAS. Introduces problem solving, algorithm development, and programming in a high-level language.

title: Programming Fundamentals 2
description: Second course in programming fundamentals. Critical-tracking course. Builds on COP 3502C with object-oriented programming and more complex data structures.

title: Applications of Discrete Structures
description: Discrete mathematics for computer science including logic, sets, graphs, and combinatorics. Critical-tracking course.

title: Data Structures and Algorithm
description: Fundamental data structures and algorithms. Critical-tracking course required by semester 7 of critical tracking.

title: Introduction to Computer Organization
description: Computer organization and architecture. Required major core course in UF CSC BS CLAS program.

title: Operating Systems
description: Principles of operating systems including processes, memory management, and file systems. Critical-tracking course due by semester 8. Prerequisite for many upper-division systems electives.

title: Introduction to Software Engineering
description: Software engineering principles, design, and team development practices. Required major core per model semester plan semester four.

title: Information and Database Systems 1
description: Database design, relational models, SQL, and information systems. Required major core.

title: Programming Language Concepts
description: Programming language paradigms, semantics, and implementation concepts.

title: Algorithm Abstraction and Design
description: Advanced algorithm design and analysis techniques.

title: Senior Project
description: Capstone senior design project for computer science majors. Exit interview required in final semester.

title: Special Topics in Computer and Information Science
description: Special topics elective. Examples include machine learning, computer networks, and computer security depending on semester.

title: Computer Networks
description: Computer networking principles and protocols. Popular major elective after operating systems.

title: Analytic Geometry and Calculus 1
description: First course in calculus. Critical-tracking and general education mathematics requirement.

title: Analytic Geometry and Calculus 2
description: Second course in calculus. Critical-tracking course due by semester 3.

title: Analytic Geometry and Calculus 3
description: Multivariable calculus. Critical-tracking course due by semester 4.

title: Computational Linear Algebra
description: Linear algebra with computational emphasis. Foundational requirement; alternative to MAS 4105.

title: Engineering Statistics
description: Statistics for engineering and science students. Foundational coursework requirement.

title: Physics with Calculus 1
description: First semester physics with calculus. Critical-tracking; paired with PHY 2048L laboratory.

title: Laboratory for Physics with Calculus 1
description: Laboratory component for PHY 2048.

title: Physics with Calculus 2
description: Second semester physics with calculus. Critical-tracking due by semester 5. Prerequisite for COP 4600 planning.

title: Laboratory for Physics with Calculus 2
description: Laboratory component for PHY 2049.

title: Professional Communication for Engineers
description: Technical and professional writing for engineering and computing students. Foundational writing requirement for CS majors who completed ENC 1101.
